analyst_note: Q2 2025: Victim count corrected sharply from Cl0p-inflated Q1 (1,607 — Rapid7 'boom and bust' characterization). LockBit breached in May 2025 — affiliate panel database leaked by unknown actors, effectively ending operational capacity. DragonForce announced cartel model transformation (March 2025, active Q2). Coveware Q2 2025 reported record average payouts driven by social engineering and data exfiltration. LockBit May breach is a significant ecosystem event.

a1._label: Russian Domestic Actions Against Ecosystem Actors
a1.notes: No documented RU domestic actions Q2 2025. LockBit May 2025 breach exposed affiliate identities — no RU domestic follow-on action. LockBitSupp (Khoroshev) remains in Russia undetained. Consistent passive tolerance across 5 consecutive quarters.

a2._label: Time Lag: Referral to Observable Russian Action

a3.notes: Populate from analyst model. LockBit breach (May 2025) by unknown non-LE actor — not a disruption of protection relationship, but exposure of affiliate identities creates pressure on protection architecture.

events.name: LockBit Database Breach — Affiliate Panel Exposure
events.notes: Unknown actor breached LockBit affiliate panel infrastructure, exposing MySQL database including affiliate identities, victim negotiation data, and operator communications. Effectively ended LockBit's operational credibility. Score: 8 — non-LE action, but ecosystem impact equivalent to major takedown; affiliate exposure at scale; LockBit operationally finished.

b1.notes: The LockBit May 2025 breach was not an LE action — score reflects ecosystem impact, not cooperation output. Intelligence value from the leaked database was significant. Affiliation with RU state unclear.

b2.notes: LockBit May breach (L2: Leaks = 1, Rep. Exposure = major event). Forum disruption elevated following breach. DragonForce cartel model attracting affiliates from disrupted groups. Affiliate loyalty shifting based on reliability rather than reputation — structural change in affiliate market. Handler complaints up (disrupted payment flows). ANALYST INFERENCE — elevated confidence given documented LockBit breach.

c1._label: Ransom Payment Volume vs. Victim Count
c1.source: Rapid7 Q2 2025 Ransomware Trends ('Boom and Bust'); Check Point Research Q2 2025 = 1,607 victims; Coveware Q2 2025 (record average payouts)
c1.notes: CREDIBILITY NOTE: Payment volume is ANALYST ESTIMATE. Victim count correction from Q1 Cl0p distortion is analytically significant — Q2 normalizes. Coveware Q2 2025 reported record average payouts ($1.1M+) driven by social engineering and data exfiltration, suggesting fewer but larger payments. Median may diverge sharply from average this quarter.

nh_reports.notes: Confirmed IC-reported payments. Median of $600K is a significant spike — consistent with Coveware Q2 2025 reporting of record average payouts. Subset only — not representative of full ecosystem payment volume.

c2.notes: Payment rate overall around 23-25% in this period. Populate from Coveware Q2 2025 quarterly report. Coveware noted rise of data exfiltration-only attacks without encryption — these may not appear in victim counts but drive payment.

c3.source: Check Point Research Q2 2025; Rapid7 Q2 2025
c3.notes: Post volume down 30% from Q1 — primarily reflects removal of Cl0p Cleo distortion, not actual attack decline. No major LE-initiated leak site takedowns in Q2. LockBit breach (non-LE) effectively ended that group's posting activity. DragonForce cartel model changed leak site dynamics — affiliates operating under their own brands.
